title: Fixture: DoD AI Predictive Maintenance
sourceText:
SAMPLE FIXTURE — FOR DEMONSTRATION ONLY

Solicitation No. FA8604-26-R-3107. The Department of the Air Force requests proposals for an artificial-intelligence predictive-maintenance capability supporting aircraft ground equipment. NAICS 541715 applies. The contemplated award is a cost-plus-fixed-fee contract with a 24-month period of performance. Proposals are due September 8, 2026.

The contractor shall ingest equipment telemetry, maintenance records, and supply data to train and operate machine-learning models that predict component failure. The contractor must document model architecture, training data provenance, performance thresholds, false-positive rates, monitoring procedures, and human-override controls. The system shall expose documented APIs and export data in nonproprietary formats. The contractor shall deliver a model card, data-management plan, quarterly performance report, bias and impact assessment, and transition plan designed to mitigate vendor lock-in.

Performance will require processing Controlled Unclassified Information on contractor information systems. DFARS 252.204-7012, 252.204-7020, 252.204-7021, and 252.204-7025 apply. The required status is CMMC Level 2 (C3PAO), current at time of award. Offerors must provide the applicable CMMC UID and evidence of a current assessment. Personnel accessing the operational environment must be U.S. citizens and eligible for a Secret clearance.

The technical proposal is limited to 35 pages and must include a security volume, staffing matrix, and two past-performance examples of comparable scale. The Government will evaluate mission effectiveness, cybersecurity, AI performance and transparency, staffing, past performance, and cost. Technical factors are significantly more important than cost.
